claim: LLMs default to hallucinating answers instead of reporting failures like CAPTCHAs or blocked pages to satisfy users
actionable: Implement explicit error handling and validation in agent workflows; don't assume agents will report access failures
source_title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data

claim: Cloudflare blocks AI crawling on approximately 20% of the web by default; AI Labyrinth traps bots with fake data
actionable: Expect web access limitations when building agents; use anti-bot bypass tools or human-like request patterns
source_title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data

claim: Agents without real web access fall back to training data (from 2024) and report it as current information
actionable: Verify agent claims by checking if they have actual web search capability; assume data may be stale without confirmation
source_title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data

claim: 60% of ChatGPT citations don't work (404 errors or non-existent pages)
actionable: Always verify agent citations before trusting them in production; don't assume cited links are real or accessible
source_title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data

claim: Web MCP with 66+ tools including real search engines, markdown scraping, CAPTCHA solving, and remote browsers can achieve 5/5 success vs 0/5 without
actionable: Integrate proper web access MCPs into agent stacks instead of relying on built-in LLM capabilities
source_title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data

claim: Free MCP tier provides 5,000 requests per month for MVP development and experimentation
actionable: Use free tier web MCPs to validate agent designs before committing to paid tiers
